channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Karachi Experiences First Monsoon Showers with Light to Moderate Rainfall
item.description: Karachi, the largest city in Pakistan, witnessed its first monsoon showers of the season on Friday, with light to moderate rain reported in various localities. According to Dawn News, areas such as Az...

item.title: Sarah Martins says she prefers helping poor people instead of paying tithe to wealthy pastors
item.description: Nollywood actress and content creator Sarah Martins has explained why she chooses not to pay tithe, saying she believes the money can make a bigger difference when it is used to help people in need di...

item.title: Andy Burnham's First Week as Prime Minister: Challenges and Political Shifts
item.description: The article examines Andy Burnham's first week in power as UK prime minister, highlighting his efforts to establish a new administration and address pressing domestic and international issues. Key dev...

item.title: Report says expanded immigration 'mega master' court hearings are creating overcrowding and due process concerns
item.description: A report by the Chicago Sun-Times examines the Trump administration's recently introduced 'mega master' immigration court hearings, a scheduling system designed to process significantly larger numbers...

item.title: Ghanaian Petition to ICC Over Xenophobic Violence in South Africa
item.description: Two Ghanaian nationals have petitioned the International Criminal Court (ICC) to investigate xenophobic violence against migrants in South Africa, arguing that the attacks may constitute crimes agains...

item.title: Tanzania boosts efforts to restore Mount Kilimanjaro
item.description: The article discusses the recent progress in the Mount Kilimanjaro restoration project, highlighting government and environmental organizations' collaborative efforts to preserve the mountain's ecosys...

item.title: Trump Reacts to Canada's Bridge Ceremony Snub Amid Trade Tensions
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump criticized Canadian officials for disinviting U.S. representatives from the opening ceremony of the Gordie Howe Bridge in Michigan, framing the decision as a consequence of...

item.title: P3.4 Million Worth of Shabu Confiscated from Terror-Linked Dealer in Lanao del Sur
item.description: Philippine authorities seized P3.4 million worth of shabu from a trafficker linked to remnants of a defunct terror group in Lanao del Sur. The operation, conducted by the Police Regional Office-Bangsa...

item.title: Geelong Secures Victory Over Melbourne Despite Injuries
item.description: In a thrilling AFL match at the MCG, Geelong (the Cats) delivered a decisive victory over Melbourne (the Demons) by 20 points, boosting their top-six hopes. The game saw Geelong dominate field positio...

item.description: This article presents a scientific study focusing on the analytical solution to the multilayer boundary value problem in electromagnetic field analysis, specifically addressing the unique challenges p...

item.title: Alberta man charged with excessive speeding on B.C. highway
item.description: A man from Alberta was arrested by police for allegedly driving at speeds exceeding three times the posted limit on a British Columbia highway. The incident, reported by CTV News, highlights concerns ...

item.title: Magnolia Secures Victory Against Meralco in PBA Match
item.description: The Philippine Basketball Association (PBA) saw a thrilling match on July 24, 2026, where the Magnolia Hotshots emerged victorious against the Meralco Bolts. The game, part of the Governors' Cup tourn...

item.title: Heavy rainfall and thunderstorms expected in Karachi and across Pakistan as PMD issues warnings
item.description: The Pakistan Meteorological Department (PMD) has issued forecasts of widespread rain, thunderstorms, and windstorms across southern and central Pakistan from July 25 to 27, with Karachi expected to ex...

item.title: Acer Chromebook Spin 514 on Sale with Additional Tech Deals
item.description: The article highlights a significant discount on Acer's Chromebook Plus Spin 514, originally priced at $749, now available for $559 at Best Buy. This 2-in-1 device features 12GB RAM, a MediaTek Kompan...

item.title: Voters Overestimate Gas Price Increases, Impacting Midterm Elections
item.description: A POLITICO analysis reveals that three in five Americans overestimate the rise in gas prices in their area, with significant implications for the upcoming midterms. The survey, conducted by Public Fir...

item.title: Tulia Recognizes Burkina Faso's Anti-Terror Efforts
item.description: The article discusses international recognition of Burkina Faso's efforts in combating terrorism. While the original text appears to be a placeholder or incomplete, the core message revolves around ac...

item.title: Three Terrorists Get Life Jail For Abducting Students In Oyo State
item.description: The Federal High Court in Abuja has sentenced three men to life imprisonment for their roles in abducting schoolchildren and teachers in Oriire Local Government Area of Oyo State. The defendants, Abdu...

item.title: Richmond Hill Vehicle Fire Under Investigation for Arson
item.description: A fire that damaged two vehicles in Richmond Hill is being investigated by York Regional Police as a potential arson incident. Fire crews were called to a home on Gatcombe Circle in the Regent Street ...

item.title: Rural Entrepreneurs Access Loans to Develop Fuel Stations
item.description: A recent initiative by the Tanzanian government has enabled rural entrepreneurs to access financial support for establishing fuel stations across the country. This development aims to boost economic g...

item.title: MEA condemns China's illegal projects in Jammu and Kashmir
item.description: The Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) has strongly condemned China's ongoing illegal projects in Jammu and Kashmir, accusing the country of violating territorial integrity. The MEA statement highligh...
